---
title: "1.5 Casting and Ranges of Values"
subtitle: "Using Objects and Methods"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Casting

### Changing the type of a value

In Java, <span class="term">casting</span> (类型转换) means converting a value from one type to another.

![Bronze casting analogy](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-5-casting-and-ranges-of-values/bronze-casting.jpg){fig-align="center" width="36%"}

The cast operator is written before an expression, such as <span class="mark">`(int)`</span> or <span class="mark">`(double)`</span>.

## Casting

### Widening and narrowing

```java
(double) 3 / 4   // 0.75
(int) 3.6        // 3
```

::: {.tight-list}
- casting `int` to `double` is a <span class="term">widening conversion</span>
- casting `double` to `int` is a <span class="term">narrowing conversion</span>
- narrowing removes the digits to the right of the decimal point
:::

Truncating is <span class="mark">not</span> the same thing as rounding.

## Casting and Division

### The cast changes the whole expression result

```java
System.out.println(3 / 4);          // 0
System.out.println(3.0 / 4);        // 0.75
System.out.println((double) 3 / 4); // 0.75
System.out.println((int) 3.0 / 4);  // 0
```

If any part of the expression becomes `double`, the expression evaluates as `double`.

## Rounding

### Round first, then cast

To round a positive `double` to the nearest `int`:

```java
int nearestInt = (int) (number + 0.5);
```

For negative values:

```java
int nearestNegInt = (int) (negNumber - 0.5);
```

This slide matters because many students assume Java rounds automatically. It does not.

## Range of Values

### Types are limited by storage

::: {.tight-list}
- `int` uses <span class="mark">32 bits</span>
- `double` uses <span class="mark">64 bits</span>
- `Integer.MAX_VALUE` is `2147483647`
- `Integer.MIN_VALUE` is `-2147483648`
:::

If a value is too large or too small for `int`, an <span class="term">integer overflow</span> (整数溢出) occurs.

## Range of Values

### Precision is also limited

`double` values can hold decimals, but not unlimited precision.

::: {.tight-list}
- repeating decimals are cut off after about <span class="mark">14-15 digits</span>
- computers may produce <span class="term">round-off error</span> when exact precision cannot be stored
- formatting output and intentional rounding can control what students see
:::

This is a good place to distinguish <span class="mark">representation limits</span> from logic mistakes.

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- division with and without casts
- truncation versus rounding checks
- integer overflow repair task
- <span class="term">1.5.4 Coding Challenge: Average 3 Numbers</span>
:::

The average challenge is the right place to insist on casting before division.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- define a <span class="term">cast</span> as converting a value to another type
- explain why `(double) total / count` avoids truncating division
- distinguish truncation from rounding
- identify `Integer.MAX_VALUE` and overflow as range limits
- note that `double` values are precise only up to a limited number of digits
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
